description: CPU-only refinement of e-0014 over data/deep (1,261 decls): split the recursive named-have count into TOP-LEVEL FRONTIER WIDTH (named have-nodes at the root antichain = the section-predictor's one-shot emission target) vs NAMED NESTING DEPTH (deepest level a named have occurs = available granularity levels) vs MAX-LEVEL WIDTH (widest antichain anywhere). Named node = name != '_' and non-empty type, matching e-0012/e-0014. Reproduces e-0014's 67.4% total-named figure as a control.
motivation: e-0014 (a-0014) re-opened q-0009 by counting named have-nodes RECURSIVELY over data/deep (67% >=2). That count conflates frontier WIDTH (the antichain a one-shot section-predictor must emit, q-0009) with nesting DEPTH (the granularity spectrum, whose shallowness — corpus_v3 max depth 3 — forced q-0005's binary recalibration). The richest decl (26 named haves) has only 3 top-level nodes, so its supply is depth, not width. Separate the two before recommending Modal spend on a predictor.
formal_results: 1,261 decls. TOP-LEVEL WIDTH: >=2 = 633 (50.2%), >=3 = 384 (30.5%), >=4 = 231 (18.3%); among nonzero mean 2.56, median 2, max 22. MAX-LEVEL WIDTH (widest antichain): >=2 = 717 (56.9%), >=3 = 451 (35.8%). NESTING DEPTH: dist 0->134, 1->520, 2->536, 3->62, 4->7, 5->2; >=2 = 607 (48.1%), >=3 = only 71 (5.6%), >=4 = 9 (0.7%); median 2, max 5. TOTAL-NAMED control reproduces e-0014 exactly (850, 67.4% >=2). Wide targets: not_minimal width 22 depth 2; psp_from_prime_psp width 20 depth 2.
results_description: data/deep's re-opened supply is WIDTH, not DEPTH. ~50% of decls (633) have a >=2-wide top-level named frontier — ample one-shot training signal for q-0009's section-predictor (vs ~27 on corpus_v3). But nesting depth stays shallow: median 2, max 5, only 5.6% reach depth>=3. So a-0014's 'ample supply' is supply for a WIDER predictor target, not for a deeper granularity spectrum.
conclusions: q-0009 (section-predictor, Variant B) IS unblocked by data/deep: 633 decls with a >=2 top-level antichain is enough to SFT a one-shot frontier predictor. But q-0005's binary rough-vs-fine recalibration STANDS even on the deep corpus — depth>=3 is only 5.6%, so there is still no room for a multi-level / U-shape granularity contrast. a-0014 should be read as 'wide-frontier supply'; the concrete next step for q-0009 is Modal round-trip verification of the 633 wide-frontier decls (a real but now precisely-scoped pipeline spend), not a multi-level redesign.
